description: Master index of canonical sources for the LLM theory KB. Schema: paper-key -> metadata + KB cross-references. excerpts_file and notes_referenced_by are populated as KB content is written. local_file is null for papers not yet downloaded (Phase 2 will populate as work proceeds).

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.authors: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser, Polosukhin
papers.summary: Introduces the Transformer: encoder-decoder architecture based entirely on attention. Defines scaled dot-product attention, multi-head attention, sinusoidal positional encoding, position-wise FFN. Base model: d=512, h=8, N=6, d_ff=2048.

papers.title: Improving Language Understanding by Generative Pre-Training
papers.summary: GPT-1: first decoder-only transformer for language modeling. 12 layers, d=768, 12 heads, d_ff=3072. Uses learned positional embeddings, GELU activation, BPE tokenization.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.summary: GPT-3: scales decoder-only transformer to 175B parameters. Demonstrates few-shot learning via in-context examples.

papers.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
papers.summary: Modern decoder-only architecture: pre-normalization with RMSNorm, SwiGLU activation (dim 2/3 * 4d), RoPE at every layer. Sizes 7B-65B on public data.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
papers.summary: Introduces BPE for NLP tokenization. Iteratively merges most frequent adjacent character pairs.

papers.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
papers.summary: RoPE: encodes position by rotating Q and K vectors via rotation matrix; dot product depends only on relative position.

papers.title: GLU Variants Improve Transformer
papers.summary: SwiGLU/GEGLU/GeGLU: GLU variants in FFN. 3 weight matrices instead of 2, inner dim reduced 2/3 to match parameter count.

papers.title: On Layer Normalization in the Transformer Architecture
papers.summary: Pre-LN vs Post-LN gradient analysis via mean-field theory. Pre-LN scales as O(d√(ln d / L)) enabling training without warmup.

papers.title: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization
papers.summary: RMSNorm: drops mean-centering from LayerNorm; 7-64% faster runtime. Standard in modern LLMs.

papers.title: PaLM: Scaling Language Modeling with Pathways
papers.summary: PaLM 540B; SwiGLU, RoPE, parallel attention/FFN layers, multi-query attention for inference efficiency.

papers.title: Mistral 7B
papers.summary: Mistral 7B: GQA + sliding-window attention. Outperforms LLaMA 2 13B at smaller size.

papers.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
papers.summary: T5: encoder-decoder text-to-text. SentencePiece + relative positional bias conventions.

papers.title: GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models from Multi-Head Checkpoints
papers.authors: Ainslie, Lee-Thorp, de Jong, Zemlyanskiy, Lebrón, Sanghai
papers.summary: Grouped-Query Attention: share K/V across head groups. KV-cache reduction; now universal in production LLMs.

papers.title: DeepSeek-V2: A Strong, Economical, and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
papers.summary: Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA): 93.3% KV-cache reduction vs MHA, outperforms GQA. Combined with DeepSeekMoE: 236B-A21B. Foundational for V3/V3.2/V4.

papers.title: DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
papers.summary: 671B-A37B MoE with MLA + DeepSeekMoE. First validated FP8 training at extreme scale (fine-grained 128x128 weight blocks, 1x128 activation vectors). Auxiliary-loss-free load balancing. Multi-token prediction (MTP) heads. DualPipe cross-node communication overlap. 2.788M H800 GPU-hours on 14.8T tokens.

papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: GRPO with verifiable binary rewards. AIME 2024 15.6%->71%. Cold-start + RL pipeline. 800K distillation set (600K reasoning, 200K non-reasoning) enables small models 1.5B-32B near-o1. Triggered the RLVR surge.

papers.title: The Llama 3 Herd of Models
papers.summary: Llama 3 405B + 70B + 8B. 15T training tokens. Post-training: SFT -> rejection sampling -> DPO (no PPO at scale). Tied tokenizer 128k vocab. ~1875 tokens/parameter (over-trained vs Chinchilla).

papers.title: The Llama 4 Herd: Architecture, Training, Evaluation, and Deployment Notes
papers.summary: MoE + iRoPE (3 RoPE layers + 1 NoPE layer interleaved with temperature scaling) + early-fusion multimodal. Extrapolates 256K training -> 10M context inference. FP8 pre-training of 2T+ tokens on 32K GPUs at 390 TFLOPs/GPU. MetaP for cross-scale HP transfer.

papers.title: Qwen3 Technical Report
papers.summary: 235B-A22B MoE + 6 dense models. 128 experts, 8 activated, no shared experts (vs DeepSeek), global-batch load-balance loss. Thinking/non-thinking mode toggle via system prompt. 4-stage post-training including GRPO.

papers.title: Qwen2.5 Technical Report
papers.summary: 1M+ SFT examples; multistage SFT+DPO+GRPO post-training; 18T pretraining tokens. Detail-rich reference for SOTA open post-training.

papers.title: Gemma 3 Technical Report
papers.summary: 1-27B. Interleaved local/global attention (1:5 ratio). SigLIP2 vision. BOND/WARM/WARP RL post-training; multi-reward (math, code, safety, multilingual). Distillation from large IT teacher.

papers.title: Gemini 2.5 Technical Report
papers.summary: Sparse MoE; 1M+ context; native multimodal; TPUv5p. Configurable thinkingBudget API for test-time compute. Architecture details intentionally opaque (no parameter count disclosed).

papers.title: OLMo 2 Furious
papers.summary: Fully transparent training (weights+data+code+logs). Peri-LN (norm before+after sublayer) + QK-Norm + RMSNorm reordering. Dolmino Mix 1124 late-stage curriculum injected during LR annealing.

papers.title: Phi-4 Technical Report
papers.summary: 14B model trained primarily on synthetic data (400B tokens from 50+ pipelines: multi-agent, self-revision, instruction reversal). Surpasses teacher GPT-4 on STEM QA. tiktoken vocab 100k.

papers.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
papers.summary: FlashAttention-1: IO-aware tiling. Computes exact attention without materializing the N×N matrix.

papers.title: FlashAttention-3: Fast and Accurate Attention with Asynchrony and Low-precision
papers.authors: Shah, Bikshandi, Zhang, Thakkar, Ramani, Dao
papers.summary: FlashAttention-3 on H100 Hopper: 740 TFLOPs FP16, ~1.2 PFLOPs FP8. ~1.5-2× FA-2.

papers.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
papers.summary: Mamba-1: selective SSM with content-aware recurrence. Linear-time alternative to attention.

papers.title: Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models and Efficient Algorithms Through Structured State Space Duality
papers.summary: Mamba-2 / SSD: unifying SSM and attention via structured state-space duality. 2-8× faster than Mamba-1.

papers.title: Jamba: A Hybrid Transformer-Mamba Language Model
papers.summary: First production-scale hybrid: MoE + Mamba + Attention layers interleaved.

papers.title: Native Sparse Attention: Hardware-Aligned and Natively Trainable Sparse Attention
papers.summary: Hardware-aligned trainable sparse attention with coarse + fine hierarchy. 64k-seq speedup over full attention.

papers.title: Differential Transformer
papers.summary: Differential attention: subtracts two softmax maps. Sparser patterns; less hallucination.

papers.title: TransMLA: Multi-Head Latent Attention Is All You Need
papers.summary: Convert GQA pretrained models to MLA post-hoc with 0.3-0.6% fine-tuning data.

papers.title: LongRoPE2: Near-Lossless LLM Context Window Scaling
papers.summary: Near-lossless context extension; 80× more token-efficient than Meta's prior approach. Preserves 98.5% short-context performance.

papers.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity
papers.summary: Switch Transformer: top-1 routing; expert capacity. Foundational MoE for trillion-parameter scale.

papers.title: DeepSeekMoE: Towards Ultimate Expert Specialization in Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
papers.summary: Fine-grained expert segmentation + shared experts. Foundational pattern for V2/V3.

papers.title: Mixtral of Experts
papers.summary: Mixtral 8x7B: open sparse MoE baseline. Top-2 routing.

papers.title: Peri-LN: Revisiting Normalization Layer in the Transformer Architecture
papers.summary: Peri-LN: normalize before AND after each sublayer. Adopted in Gemma and OLMo families. More stable than Pre-LN.

papers.title: Byte Latent Transformer: Patches Scale Better Than Tokens
papers.summary: Tokenizer-free BLT: dynamic byte patches. Matches LLaMA 3 at 50% fewer inference FLOPs at 8B scale.

papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
papers.authors: Rafailov, Sharma, Mitchell, Ermon, Manning, Finn
papers.summary: DPO: closed-form reformulation of RLHF as classification on preferences. Removes need for explicit reward model and PPO.

papers.title: SimPO: Simple Preference Optimization with a Reference-Free Reward
papers.summary: Average log-prob as implicit reward; length normalization; no reference model. +6.4 AlpacaEval 2 over DPO.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.summary: Foundational CAI: SL-CAI + RL-CAI pipeline. RLAIF replacing human preference labels.

papers.title: Tülu 3: Pushing Frontiers in Open Language Model Post-Training
papers.summary: Fully open 4-stage recipe: data curation -> SFT -> DPO -> RLVR. Beats Llama 3.1-Instruct, Qwen 2.5, GPT-4o-mini. Tülu 3 405B (Jan 2025) scales RLVR.

papers.title: Does Reinforcement Learning Really Incentivize Reasoning Capacity in LLMs Beyond the Base Model?
papers.summary: RLVR improves sampling efficiency (pass@1) on problems base model can already solve, but does NOT expand reasoning capacity. Capability ceiling set at pre-training. Inverts GRPO-elicits-reasoning narrative.

papers.title: Muon is Scalable for LLM Training
papers.summary: Muon (Newton-step momentum + gradient orthogonalization) at LLM scale. ~2× AdamW compute efficiency. Trains Moonlight 3B/16B-MoE on 5.7T tokens.

papers.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM Reinforcement Learning System at Scale
papers.summary: Decoupled Clip and Dynamic sampling Policy Optimization. AIME 2024 50pts on Qwen-32B; 50% fewer steps than GRPO baseline. verl framework open-sourced.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.summary: Original CoT paper: eliciting step-by-step reasoning via prompting. Baseline for all subsequent reasoning work.

papers.title: Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally Can Be More Effective than Scaling Model Parameters
papers.authors: Snell, Lee, Xu, Kumar (UC Berkeley / Google DeepMind)
papers.summary: Formalizes inference-time compute as a new scaling axis. Smaller model + more inference budget can beat 14× larger model on reasoning tasks.

papers.title: s1: Simple Test-Time Scaling
papers.summary: 1K curated examples + budget forcing (appending 'Wait' tokens) reproduces o1-preview test-time scaling curves.

papers.title: Process Reward Models That Think
papers.summary: Generative CoT verifier using 1% of PRM800K labels. Outperforms discriminative PRMs on ProcessBench and AIME'24.

papers.title: rStar-Math: Small LLMs Can Master Math Reasoning with Self-Evolved Deep Thinking
papers.summary: Four-cycle self-evolution with MCTS+PRM. Qwen2.5-Math-7B -> 90% MATH; rivals o1-mini at 7B scale.

papers.title: KVQuant: Towards 10 Million Context Length LLM Inference with KV Cache Quantization
papers.summary: Per-channel KV quantization + nuq2. 8x compression, 1M tokens on A100.

papers.title: The Era of 1-bit LLMs: All Large Language Models are in 1.58 Bits
papers.summary: BitNet b1.58: ternary {-1,0,+1} weights match FP16 at scale. Foundational claim for native 1-bit training.

papers.title: BitNet b1.58 2B4T Technical Report
papers.summary: First open-source native 1-bit LLM at 2B scale (4T training tokens). Matches FP16 accuracy with 2-6× CPU speedup. Deployable, not POC.

papers.title: EAGLE-3: Scaling up Inference Acceleration via Training-Time Test
papers.summary: Multi-layer feature fusion; abandons feature prediction. Up to 6.5× speedup; 1.4× over EAGLE-2.

papers.title: XGrammar: Flexible and Efficient Structured Generation Engine for LLMs
papers.summary: Up to 100× speedup. Default backend in vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM. Splits vocab into context-independent (precomputed) vs context-dependent (runtime) tokens.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.summary: Foundational power-law scaling: loss vs params, data, compute. Spawned the entire scaling-law literature.

papers.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
papers.summary: Chinchilla: tokens ≈ 20× params at compute-optimum. Overthrew Kaplan's model-size bias.

papers.title: Tensor Programs V: Tuning Large Neural Networks via Zero-Shot Hyperparameter Transfer
papers.summary: μP: maximal-update parametrization for zero-shot HP transfer across model sizes.

papers.title: u-μP: The Unit-Scaled Maximal Update Parametrization
papers.summary: Combines μP with unit-scaling. Enables native FP8 training without dynamic rescaling.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Precision
papers.summary: Precision-aware scaling laws. Post-training quantization has increasing cost as training extends — actively harmful past a threshold.

papers.title: In-context Learning and Induction Heads
papers.summary: Induction heads as core ICL mechanism. Canonical Transformer Circuits Thread paper.

papers.title: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 Small
papers.summary: IOI circuit: 26 attention heads with functional roles. Mech-interp case study.

papers.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT (ROME)
papers.summary: Causal tracing localizes facts in MLP layers. Introduced activation patching to broader community.

papers.title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 Sonnet
papers.summary: SAEs scaled to production Claude 3 Sonnet. Discovers abstract, safety-relevant features.

papers.title: Gemma Scope: Open Sparse Autoencoders Everywhere All At Once on Gemma 2
papers.summary: Open SAE release: 400+ SAEs across all layers of Gemma 2 2B/9B/27B. Introduces JumpReLU.

papers.title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models
papers.summary: Cross-layer transcoders + attribution graphs. Applied to production Claude 3.5 Haiku.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Features
papers.summary: Critical paper: different SAE training runs find different bases. Challenges monosemanticity assumption. Active debate.

papers.title: Eliciting Latent Predictions from Transformers with the Tuned Lens
papers.summary: Trained affine translators per layer. More reliable than raw logit lens.

papers.title: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding
papers.summary: Original MMLU. Now near-saturated (frontier models ~90%+).

papers.title: MMLU-Pro: A More Robust and Challenging Multi-Task Language Understanding Benchmark
papers.summary: 12k graduate-level questions, 10-answer MCQ. Better discriminates frontier models than MMLU.

papers.title: GPQA: A Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A Benchmark
papers.summary: GPQA Diamond: 198 PhD-expert ~65% questions. Now surpassed by frontier AI by large margin.

papers.title: FrontierMath: A Benchmark for Evaluating Advanced Mathematical Reasoning in AI
papers.summary: Novel research-grade math; <2% AI solve rate at launch. 60+ expert mathematicians.

papers.title: Humanity's Last Exam
papers.summary: 2,500 expert-vetted cross-domain questions; multimodal. Current SOTA ~65%.

papers.title: SWE-bench Verified
papers.summary: 500 human-validated GitHub issue resolution tasks. Current SOTA Claude Sonnet 4.5 at 77.2%.

papers.title: A Watermark for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Green/red token list watermarking. Canonical LLM watermarking paper.

papers.title: Scalable Watermarking for Identifying Large Language Model Outputs (SynthID-Text)
papers.summary: Tournament-sampling watermark; open-sourced; deployed in Gemini App. Nature publication 2024.

papers.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization: Eliciting Strong Capabilities with Weak Supervision
papers.authors: Burns, Izmailov, Kirchner et al. (OpenAI Superalignment)
papers.summary: Core scalable oversight question: GPT-2 supervisor recovers near GPT-3.5 performance.

papers.title: Alignment Faking in Large Language Models
papers.summary: First empirical demonstration of alignment faking without explicit training. Claude 3 Opus complies with harmful queries 14% when it infers it's in training.

papers.title: Frontier Models Are Capable of In-Context Scheming
papers.summary: o1, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 405B all exhibit scheming. First systematic eval.

papers.title: HarmBench: A Standardized Evaluation Framework for Automated Red Teaming and Robust Refusal
papers.summary: 510 behaviors, 18 attack methods, 33 models. Standard red-teaming benchmark; used by AISI.

papers.title: FineWeb: Decanting the Web for the Finest Text Data at Scale
papers.summary: 15T-token CC-derived dataset; proxy-model validation for filter selection. Spawned FineWeb-Edu educational classifier.

papers.title: Data Mixing Laws: Optimizing Data Mixtures by Predicting Language Modeling Performance
papers.summary: Predictable functional relationship between domain proportions and loss. 48% step savings from optimized mixture.

papers.title: TorchTitan: One-Stop PyTorch Native Solution for Production-Ready LLM Pre-training
papers.summary: Reference implementation for 4D parallelism (FSDP2 + TP + PP + Context Parallel). Ring Attention for 1M-sequence training. 65% speedup on Llama 3.1 8B.

papers.title: Arcee MergeKit: A Toolkit for Merging Large Language Models
papers.summary: Open-source toolkit: TIES, DARE, Task Arithmetic, SLERP, Frankenmerge. Backbone of community model merging.

papers.title: Ring Attention with Blockwise Transformers for Near-Infinite Context
papers.summary: Ring attention: distribute attention across devices for sequence length scaling.

papers.title: Leave No Context Behind: Efficient Infinite Context Transformers with Infini-attention
papers.summary: Compressive memory + local + linear attention in single block. 1M-passkey.

papers.title: RWKV-7 'Goose' with Expressive Dynamic State Evolution
papers.summary: Generalized delta rule with vector-valued gating. Claims state-tracking expressiveness beyond Transformers under standard complexity assumptions.

papers.title: Visual Instruction Tuning
papers.summary: LLaVA: vision encoder + MLP projector + LLM paradigm. Foundational adapter-based MLLM.

papers.title: InternVL3: Exploring Advanced Training and Test-Time Recipes
papers.summary: Native multimodal pretraining (joint image+text from scratch). SOTA open MLLM.

papers.title: Fast Transformer Decoding: One Write-Head is All You Need
papers.summary: Introduces Multi-Query Attention (MQA): all heads share a single K, V projection, only Q is per-head. Identifies KV-cache bandwidth as the dominant cost of incremental Transformer decoding (memory/compute ratio Theta(n/d + 1/b)). Cuts KV cache by factor h with minor quality cost.

papers.title: FlashAttention-2: Faster Attention with Better Parallelism and Work Partitioning
papers.summary: Re-partitions FlashAttention work so more of the algorithm runs as matmul ops (better tensor-core utilization). Reduces non-matmul FLOPs. ~2x speedup over FlashAttention-1 on A100.

papers.title: Native Sparse Attention: Hardware-Aligned and Natively Trainable Sparse Attention
papers.summary: DeepSeek-team proposal for natively sparse attention trained end-to-end (sparsity is part of pre-training, not retrofitted). Hardware-aligned for long-context efficiency. Claims to match dense attention quality at 64k+ context with reduced compute.
